entry.title: Re: MDG document storeage
entry.content:
Hi 

In SAP, documents related to change requests are typically stored in the SAP Content Server or a linked Document Management System (DMS). The table USMD1211 links documents to change requests, but the physical storage is usually managed externally (e.g., in DMS tables like `DRAD` for attachments). To download documents in bulk, you can use SAP's Archive Link or a custom ABAP program that accesses the DMS content.

you can refer to the following SAP documentation:
SAP Content Server Documentation: http://help.sap.com/viewer/product/SAP_CONTENT_SERVER

SAP Archive Link Documentation: http://help.sap.com/viewer/product/SAP_ARCHIVELINK

SAP Document Management System (DMS): http://help.sap.com/viewer/product/SAP_DOCUMENT_MANAGEMENT_SYSTEM

entry.title: Re: SAC Data Action: Copy data from one quarter to another quarter
Dynamic Input Form and Report
entry.content:
Hi Team, I want to deliver this mini project. The report should include a side-by-side input form and a report, with only date and version selection. Currently, this is designed in Excel. Below are the points that need to be considered before designing. I need your help in modeling and report design.

Input Form

  • Type of Project: This should be a dropdown, and based on the segment master, the input form should change partially, not fully.
  • Asset Name: This should be a text input.
  • Asset Type: This should be a dropdown.

Similarly, the form should be designed accordingly.

Report

As you can see, each cell has a different formula written. I am unable to visualize how to design this.

entry.title: Re: Manual retrigger for messages flowing through TPM
entry.content:
Hi 

To reprocess the message in the dead letter queue, you need to first change its status from 'waiting' to 'failed.' This can often be done through a dedicated admin tool or by manually updating the message state in the database.

Once the status is set to 'failed,' you should be able to retrigger the message for reprocessing. Additionally, ensure there are no locks or dependencies preventing the status update.

entry.title: Service Order with Procurement of Spare Parts from Intercompany Supplier
entry.content:
In this blog you will learn about the Service Order process with Procurement of Spare Parts from Intercompany Supplier (Intercompany Stock Transport Order). This service process flow is based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, release 2023. 

Step 1: Create and Release Service Order with External Spare Part item 

Start the process by creating a service order with an External Spare Part item. Standard item category SRPM can be used. In the Involved Parties on item level you can enter a prefered vendor and enter an Intercompany Supplier. 

When releasing the service order (and save), a purchase requisition is created. The purchase requisition is visible in the Transaction History. 

In this example we first create a Purchase Requisition. It is also possible to configure the Service Order item category to immediately create a Purchase Order. 

Step 2: Convert Purchase Requisition into Purchase Order

In our example, company code 1710 is requesting this spare part from company code 1010 and plant 1010. A Supplier Business Partner is created for Plant 1010. 

The Purchase Order is created for this Intercompany Supplier and the Purchase Order is account assigned to the Service Order Item. 

Based on the Documentation described here, the Purchase Order type is NB, item category BLANK, and Delivery type NLCC. 

The Purchase Order is also visible in the Transaction History of the Service Order. 

Step 3: Outbound Delivery for Purchase Order

The Intercompany Supplier (Plant 1010) creates an Outbound Delivery based on the Purchase Order (transaction VL10B).

Step 4: Outbound Delivery - Perform Picking and Goods Issue

From the Outbound Delivery, perform picking of the spare parts in Plant 1010 and trigger Goods Issue. Standard movement type 643 is used in our example as a 2-step approach. A separate goods receipt needs to posted in the demanding company (1710).

Step 5: Goods Receipt for Outbound Delivery

Create a Goods Receipt based on the Outbound Delivery (using movement type 101). 

Step 6: Check updates in Service Order

Both goods movements (good issue and goods receipt) are added to the Transaction History of the Service Order.

Step 7: Create Intercompany Invoice

The supplying company (1010) creates an intercompany invoice towards the requesting company (1710).

Step 8: Create and post Vendor Invoice 

In the Requesting Company, create and post the Vendor Invoice from the Intercompany supplier. The vendor invoice is added to the Purchase Order history.

Step 9: Check Margin Analysis for the Service Order

In Margin Analysis for the Service Order item we see the cost postings from the Goods Receipt and correction from the Vendor Invoice.

Step 10: Create Service Confirmation

Now that the Spare Part is available in the Requesting Company, the service technician can create a service confirmation and use the spare part.

Step 11: Create Customer Invoice

When the service is finished and the service order is completed, the customer billing process can be started.

Step 12: Margin Analysis

Finally, check revenue and cost postings in the Margin Analysis for the Service Order.

entry.title: BYD2408 SAP Excel AddIn Compatibility
entry.content:
Which Office 365 Version supports the SAP ByD Excel Add-In


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2408 SAP Excel AddIn Compatibility
entry.content:
I have shared you the latest version for which it is working fine for us.(2410)

Office 365 is mentioned in the requirement page for excel add-in in the Help.

It is a subscription plan which provides license to latest version of MS office. No need to mention any version specifically.

 

Only desktop version of excel will be supported.

entry.title: SF Recruiting Druckvorschau Details zur Stellenanforderung
entry.content:
Hallo, 

statt der Druckvorschau wird folgende Fehlermeldung ausgegeben:

Das Dokument, auf das Sie zugreifen möchten, befindet sich nicht mehr in diesem Ordner.Möglicherweise wurde es bereits bearbeitet, in einen anderen Ordner verschoben oder gelöscht, oder Sie verwenden einen ungültigen Link.Klicken Sie hier, um zu Ihrer Startseite zurückzukehren.

entry.title: Business Context For Foundation Models In AI Launchpad
entry.content:
Hi 

I am trying to consume Foundation models from SAP Generative AI hub. As part of this i have created Configuration and Deployment url in SAP AI Launchpad.

I am able to access the deployment url from post man.

 

It was answering my questions , Suppose if i ask captial of England ,it gaves me London .

Now I want to use this in one of SAP based application.The answers must be based on SAP Application data.

Suppose if i ask what is name of customer no 101 ,Then it should be from SAP Business Partner Details.

Right now it was not giving information specific to SAP.

How is it linked to SAP specif data .

Can some one elobrate steps to achive this

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing: Yield Confirmation with Final Confirmation
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I know, that the Final Confirmation that is used to set operation CNF in ERP is currently deactivated in the DM Integration Workflow and as I have read, there once was a XSLT that has sent this status. Could anyone provide the old XSLT to me? If I only activate the Final Confirmation in the current XSLT, it always sends a Final Confirmation, but it only should do this, if the ordered quantity is already confirmed. I hope that this check, if the whole quantity is already confirmed, is included in the old XSLT.

Thank you!

entry.title: SAC: missing updates for KBA 3533963 ? (Table.onSelect not triggered anymore)
entry.content:
Hi,
KBA 3533963 was released on 30.10.2024 and describes that standard events like Table.onSelect are not triggered anymore as they used to be.
in my case I have 2 strories with tables where selection type is set to single cell (as a workaround for open improvement request 280582 ).
Since Q4 update users have to right-click in the table to trigger event .onSelect. Previous to update it was left-click.
will this be solved in the forseable future?
br
edgar

entry.title: Re: No JE for Use tax on Supplier invoice
entry.content:
Hi Chintan, are you using external tax system for tax determination or standard SAP. Also, review your configuration with the Use tax code setup.

Here you can find more information:

External Sales and Use Tax Calculation

Import Tax Codes

entry.title: Re: RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [3]: Creating events with deep payloads
entry.content:
Hi @MioYasutake 

triggering the event is always extremely dependent on your use case and where you want to trigger the event from. 

The following is an example where I use a local event handler to catch an event and trigger another custom one. Local event handlers are not intended for that, but it still works :D.

Global class:

CLASS z_local_bp_event_consumption DEFINITION PUBLIC ABSTRACT FINAL FOR EVENTS OF I_BusinessPartnertp_2 .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS z_local_bp_event_consumption IMPLEMENTATION.
ENDCLASS.

Local types:

CLASS lcl_abap_behv_event_handler DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_event_handler.

  PRIVATE SECTION.

    METHODS consume_changed FOR ENTITY EVENT changed_instances FOR BusinessPartner~Changed.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lcl_abap_behv_event_handler IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD consume_changed.

    cl_abap_tx=>save( ).

    LOOP AT changed_instances INTO DATA(changed_instance).

        DATA(et_events) = VALUE zbp_addr_changed=>tt_events(
        (
            businesspartner = '101'
            partnerId = '222'
            firstName = 'Martin'
            lastName = 'Lopas'
            businessPartnerType = '1'
            address = VALUE #(
                nation = ''
                city = ''
                street = 'Church Street 103'
                houseNumber = ''
                postalCode = '56265'
                country = 'US'
            )
        )
    ).

    zbp_addr_changed=>raise_address_changed( it_events = et_events ).

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

The event in this example uses a slightly different payload, but other that that the setup is exactly the same.

 

One reason why your event doesn't show in the event monitor could be because there is no topic binding for that event. The event monitor only shows events with a topic binding on that channel

entry.title: Re: BAPI Extension.
entry.content:
You can do it by below code

wa_extensionin-valuepart1 = wa_bape_vbak+0(240).

wa_extensionin-valuepart2 = wa_bape_vbak+240.

entry.title: Re: How to copy from actuals and budget to forecast in sa...
entry.content:
Hi @abe_enoch

The Date dimension member ID (ex, 202410) is not a numeric value but a string. Therefore, the date dimension member ID cannot use comparison operators, nor can addition or subtraction operations be performed. The date dimension member ID needs to be converted into a numeric value to perform comparison operators and addition or subtraction operations. One way to transform it into a unique month number is by using the formula:

  • @Month = (YEAR([d/Date])) * 12 + MONTH([d/Date])
  • Example
String Numeric  Numeric  Numeric 
Date member ID YEAR([d/Date])  Month([d/Date]) (YEAR([d/Date])) * 12 + MONTH([d/Date]) 
202401                    2,0241                                                                 24,289
202402                    2,0242                                                                 24,290
202403                    2,0243                                                                 24,291
202404                    2,0244                                                                 24,292
202405                    2,0245                                                                 24,293
202406                    2,0246                                                                 24,294
202407                    2,0247                                                                 24,295
202408                    2,0248                                                                 24,296

 

I hope this makes it clear.

Moonjun

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to uninstall /all on "On installation...
entry.content:
Hello, you, can’t do it by adding a script to "On installation Start" explanation here:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sapsetup-how-do-i-call-uninstall-sapgui-32bit-version/qaq-p/12764158/comment-id/4791651

What you can do is creating bat file and entering following 2 lines, one for Uninstall and second for install package e.g. "GUISAPx64".

\\YourSAPInstallServer\SAPInstall\Setup\NwSapSetup.exe /all /nodlg /Uninstall

\\YourSAPInstallServer\SAPInstall\Setup\NwSapSetup.exe /nodlg /Package="GUISAPx64"

regards Damian

entry.title: Powerdesigner: Create calculated collection based on attribute
entry.content:
Hi all,

This seems like kind of a basic question, but I haven't been able to find a whole lot about calculated collections.

I'm trying to create a calculated collection of objects in SAP PowerDesigner, so I can use this pre-filtered set of entities in my report. The problem is: I can't get the syntax right. The best result I've gotten so far (in the report) is a number of lists containing one entity each instead of one list containing every entity. In the dependencies tab, only one entity (the entity whose property window is open) is visible. 

This is the code I used:

Function %Collection%(obj, coll)
   If obj.GetAttribute("Stereotype") = "Relatie" Then
   coll.Add obj
   End if
   %Collection% = True
   
End Function

Can the calculated collection be used as a pre-defined filter for entities (so only entities with the stereotype "Relatie" are in it)?

 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Saprouter denies https after some time
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a pretty straightforward saprouter config file.
P * * *
and some lines
P <ip> website.domain 443

It works just fine, BUT after a few days the router starts denying https access.
I looked at all logs, nothing restarted the router or renewed its access table.

If I commit: saprouter -n
It is fine again and it is working fine for a few days.

I installed the latest version, but the problem persists.

What could be the reason?

Thanks!
I.G.

entry.title: Edmx file fails as it does not have DOCTYPE
entry.content:
Hello,

We generated ODATA v2 client for few services using this guide:
https://sap.github.io/cloud-sdk/docs/java/features/odata/vdm-generator

We use MAven depencencies and Maven plugin to generate neede classes. Nevertheless, when I build our project I've got warning and errors like:
Warning: validation was turned on but an org.xml.sax.ErrorHandler was not set, which is probably not what is desired. Parser will use a default ErrorHandler to print the first 0 errors. Please call the setErrorHandler method to fix this.
Error: URI=file:/C:/Repository/planner/integrator/S4Hana/edmx/API_CLFN_PRODUCT_SRV.xml Line=2: Document root element "edmx:Edmx", must match DOCTYPE root "null".
Error: URI=file:/C:/Repository/planner/integrator/S4Hana/edmx/API_CLFN_PRODUCT_SRV.xml Line=2: Document is invalid: no grammar found.

Same errors happened for all our edmx files. 

How could we avoid it? We would prefer to disable that default validator or use that one which understand edmx, but we did not found such option in Maven plugin.

Code part from our MAven plugin is:

<plugin>
    <groupId>com.sap.cloud.sdk.datamodel</groupId>
    <artifactId>odata-generator-maven-plugin</artifactId>
    <version>${sap.cloud.version}</version>
    <executions>
        <execution>
            <id>generate-consumption</id>
            <phase>generate-sources</phase>
            <goals>
                <goal>generate</goal>
            </goals>
            <configuration>
                <inputDirectory>${project.basedir}/edmx</inputDirectory>
                <outputDirectory>${sap.vdm.generated.folder}</outputDirectory>
                <deleteOutputDirectory>false</deleteOutputDirectory>
                <overwriteFiles>true</overwriteFiles>
                <packageName>com.delfoi.planner.integrator.customer.s4hana.s4hana.vdm</packageName>
                <compileScope>COMPILE</compileScope>
                <serviceMethodsPerEntitySet>true</serviceMethodsPerEntitySet>
            </configuration>
        </execution>
    </executions>
</plugin>

Where

<sap.cloud.version>5.14.0</sap.cloud.version>

PS: Also I found that in this tag only last link is actually accessible. First two links are "The resource you are looking for has been removed, had its name changed, or is temporarily unavailable."


<edmx:Edmx Version="1.0"
xmlns:edmx="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/06/edmx"
xmlns:m="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata"
xmlns:sap="http://www.sap.com/Protocols/SAPData">

entry.title: S/4HANA CRM - Service Order/Confirmation split valuation of service part
entry.content:
I have an issue with some spare parts that are managed with a split valuation. In the service confirmation I get the error to enter the valuation type required for the GI, or else the system will not let me finish the confirmation.

The issue is that there is no way to enter the valuation type in the service order, the service confirmation, or the reservation for the service part. Is there a way to maintain the valuation type manually, so that the system lets me complete the confirmation and issue this part?

I am aware of OSS note 3466597 that states that full support for split valuation in Service is coming with 2023 Feature Pack 2, but our system is not on that currently. Looking for a workaround.

Thank you,

Danail

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!

entry.title: Not working AI Core destinations
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm trying to discover the possibilities of CAP LLM plugin by following this tutorial on github rag-quickstart-app

The thing that I cannot pass through is configuring the BTP destination to AI Core. 

On our BTP account we have set up Ai Core Instance and deployed some models. We used them before outside from CAP so we know that Ai Core itself work properly, and models are configured fine.

When I check connection in BTP destinations it's returning green tick with 404:Not Found. When I'm trying the same connection through postman its working perfectly fine.

This is destination configuration:

In the CAP application from tutorial I'm receiving errors 404 while trying so setup connection to oauth service. I tried every SAP tutorial and blog I could find about Ai Core configurations, Destinations, and CAP services binding but nothing worked for me.

I would be really grateful for any tips about correct configurations.

Best regards
Piotr

Suggest you to read below Blog for tips of getting your answers faster: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/q-amp-a-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-how-to-get-your-answer-faster/ba-p/13934481

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!

entry.title: Query last 3 characters of pay scale group in a time PCR (length of pay scale group is not fixed)
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a requirement to check last 3 characters in a pay scale group and if it equal to '104', need to process that further. I tried using VAOFF and VALEN before OUTWPTRFGR, however, the problem is that length of pay scale group that end with 104 is not fixed i.e. some are 8 chars long, some are 7 chars long. Checking varied number of characters through VAOFF and VALEN would make the PCR messy.

Please suggest if we have a direct operation in the PCR to query certain number of characters from the end in a field or is there any other method to proceed ?

Thanks,

Shekhar

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to change the font size in tables rows?

Even if I simplify all the screens and the ALV-Layout, it would be very helpful if we could change the text-size within the ALV-Table. Header can be changed but not the content - is this correct? (SP18)

Regards, Nicolas

I would by all means avoid mingling with SAP system statuses.

entry.title: Re: plan order view
entry.content: Thank you very much for your reply.

entry.title: Re: Getting Timeout Error when adding a S4 HANA system in...
entry.content:
Hello!

Is 10.53.252.5 the actual IP address of the SAP server?

Because if NAT is involved, then the external NAT IP address must be added to the parameter "gw/alternative_hostnames".

Refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/abapconn/3354079716.html .

Regards,

Isaías

entry.title: Double budget is consumed when parking invoice with retention
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

There is an issue with budget consumption during parking invoice through MIR7 where retention is indicated. System releases budget consumption on PO level and consumes triple budget on Parked invoice (value type 60). Once parked invoice is approved and posted, system releases extra consumed budget. Field FMFIIT-MENGE (quantity) for parked invoice appears "3" instead of "1". No such issue arises when invoice is directly posted through MIRO.

entry.title: Re: secussl_read: SSL_read() failed
entry.content: Hi, is it an SAP instance that cannot connect to the Web Dispatcher? Or is it the Web Dispatcher that cannot connect to the backend SAP instance? In either case, can you share more lines of the log?

entry.title: Re: How to see the join type of a model made up of two data sources in SAC?
entry.content: So that means SAC supports only 2 joins i.e. Inner join and by default 'Left Outer Join'. Is that correct?

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Scope: What Happens after 2025/2030
entry.content:
Dear Taha Rizvi,
Anja and I work in the  same team, so please let me directly answer your question. 
LIS in EAM is part of the compatibility packages.  Please see simplification item and business impact note https://me.sap.com/notes/2267463 . We will also make an update of this blog text to be more precise.
I am sorry for the confusion the present statement created.
Best regards,
Stefan

Cheers

Jens

entry.title: Re: Damaged label in warehouse and need to print one single product label

entry.title: Re: SAP C4C: Approve Document directly through Email
entry.content: Very useful and informative blog

entry.title: Getting 403 forbidden error on accessing an OData Service from SAPUI5 App
entry.content:
hi Team,

We have consumed an OData service /sap/opu/OData/sap/zxx_Active_plant_srv/ET_EMS_AND_NON_EMS_PLANTSet?$format=json at   Custom  UI5 Application SalesOrderApp for fetching plant from Backend system s/4 hana . In our landscape we have backend system : BKX and a front end gateway server : FKX on which we host our UI5 custom apps & other standard Apps. There is a change in the landscape, as they are combining backend system and front end gateway system to one system - Embedded Fiori System : EKX 

So we are moving all the OData services and UI5 Apps to the Embedded Fiori System, By importing the TRs from Back End system : BKX  and Front End system : FKX to Embedded Fiori System : EKX. 

The OData service is working fine and fetching the active plant in Custom UI5 Application SalesOrderApp hosted on FKX , but the same OData service is throwing 403 Forbidden Error in Custom UI5 Application SalesOrderApp hosted on EKX (Embedded Fiori System). All roles and authorizations required for executing this App and OData service are maintained at EKX.  

When we excute the OData service at EKX directly via gw_client , it is working fine, but where as from UI5 App it is throwing 403 forbidden error. 

Kindly help and do the needful. 

Please find the below Error screen shot

entry.title: Re: Enabling My Inbox Fiori App in SAP Build Work Zone: Streamlining Work Item Management with SAP B
entry.content:
Hi @Ankita_Medankar12 ,

Thanks for the detailed blog.

Could you please tell me where SAP S/4 HANA private cloud my inbox will be linked to build work zone? Is it linking only process automation my inbox application?

Please help me to understand.

Thanks.

entry.title: IBP Format sheet-Approach to do Color Code formatting for Cells without Zero values in Planning View
entry.content:
Hi Experts, All,

 

In IBP Formatting sheet -

Need Approach to do Color Code formatting for Cells without Zero values in IBP Excel Add-in Planning View Template.

 

As per user requirement, we have made 'Conditional Formatting' (If Key Figure Cell Value is NOT Equal to '0' (Zero) then display in Red color for the IBP Key Figure.

This color code Formatting is done in 'IBPFormattingSheet' for the Key Figure in 'IBP DEVELOPMENT System' and tested and working fine.

 

The Question is :

Do we need to do this cell Color Code Formatting directly in Planning View Templates in IBP PRODUCTION system ?

Or is there any way/option to Transport or Export from Development system to Quality system and to Production system ?

 

Appreciate your help in this regards.

Thanks.

Parthi

Miguel

entry.title: Re: Introducing new type of Data Access Control "Operator and Value" for SAP Datasphere
entry.content: @murali_balreddy2  No , PRODUCT_GROUP = 2 is not between 10 and 20 . Nor its in EMEA and Germany . So PRODUCT_GROUP = 2 should not be seen . It filters out 1, 3, 4 etc correctly

entry.title: Re: Getting 403 forbidden error on accessing an OData Ser...
entry.content:
check su53

for odata service, there are two object iwsg iwsv for authorization, very likely you are missing one of them

entry.title: Brief Overview Stock-Specific Unit of Measure Overview In SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hi All 

Today I am writing blog for concept of SUoM in SAP EWM

Stock-Specific Unit of Measure Overview and usage

 The stock-specific unit of measure (SUoM) is a second unit of measure beside the base unit of measure, which is available in the background in the stock data and in logistic-relevant documents such as inbound and outbound deliveries and warehouse tasks. This UoM covers the data capturing during the inbound process, and the use of it in the internal and outbound processes. In the outbound process, the ERP outbound delivery can be updated with the physically picked UoM, based on the SUoM. It is deployable to the customer through a delivery note and/or invoice, so that the documents reflect the physical goods.

Activate the business function EWM SCM_EWM_FND

Effects on Master Data

If you use the stock-specific unit of measure (UoM) functionality in Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), the alternative UoM of your stock gets an important stock attribute, as it reflects the physical packaging of your products. If you change the UoM quantity conversion factors in the product master, it affects this attribute.

Note-If you would like to define new packaging sizes for your product, you should introduce new UoMs instead, so that existing stock is not affected.

Preferred Unit of Measure

The preferred unit of measure (UoM) is the packaging size in which you usually store a product in your warehouse. You can maintain the preferred UoM in the material master at a global level and in the product master at a warehouse level via transaction code /SCWM/MAT1. The system uses the global preferred UoM in warehouse processes unless you maintain a warehouse-specific one.

The Preferred UoM is used as a proposal in below Warehouse Processes

Inbound Process

Outbound Process

Physical Inventory Process

Note-You can also use the warehouse management monitor node Stock Not In Pref. UoM to identify stock which is not in the preferred UoM. In the standard SAP monitor structure you can find this node under Stock and Bin.

SUoM in Inbound Processes

 You can use stock-specific unit of measure (SUoM) functionality in inbound processes in a variety of ways, including inbound delivery, packing, quality inspection, deconsolidation and putaway.

SUoM in Outbound Processes

You can use stock-specific unit of measure (SUoM) functionality in outbound processes in a variety of ways, including picking and outbound delivery.

 

SUoM in Physical Inventory

 You can use stock-specific unit of measure (SUoM) functionality in physical inventory processes in a variety of ways.

SUoM in Cross Processes

You can use stock-specific unit of measure (SUoM) functionality in cross processes in a variety of ways, including in opportunistic cross-docking, in the warehouse management monitor, and in conjunction with radio frequency functions. You cannot use it in conjunction with catch weight functions.

 

Conclusion- This blog will you take through concept of SUOM. It’s has several benefits by implementing it.

Please see this  blog - https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-members/preferred-alternative-uom-for-warehouse-operation-in-ewm/ba-p/13679453

entry.title: Re: File transfer using SAP Integration Suite AWS Adapter
entry.content:
Hello Yuta_Takahashi,

Iam also facing the same issue and have added the certificate of s3.amazonaws.com to the integration suite tenant. But still the issue is not resolved.

Would you please share the steps here, how you resolved the issue.

entry.title: Re: Author Spotlight: Tim Koster - November 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Hello @kostertimm,
What a recognition - Author Spotlight!⭐️ Congratulations 🥳

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing 24.11 Release new capabilities
entry.content:
* This blog post is Work In Progress and will be constantly updated in the upcoming days.
Do you like this content? Please live a Kudos!
 
Add Annotations to Production Processes
 
Use the brand new "Annotation" Control to add comments and document production processes.
 
 
Assign Multiple Processors to Alerts
 
In Manage Alerts, you can select multiple users in the selected plant and then assign them as the processors of an alert. 
 

Quality Inspection Enhancements

  • New object-based role templates for quality management. These templates work with the Quality Inspection Characteristic List and the Quality Inspection Results plugins. They help you organize tasks on a more granular level:

    Quality_Management_Read
    Quality_Management_Manage

     

  • Quality Inspection support in Operation Activity PODs

    The following plugins are now available for the Operation Activity POD:

    - Quality Inspection Characteristic List, which you can use to display inspection characteristics
    - Quality Inspection Results, which you can use to record inspection results

  • Separation of SFC Completion and Inspection Results Synchronization in the Work Center POD and the Operation Activity POD

    In a future release, we will separate the SFC completion in the Work Center POD and Operation Activity POD from the synchronization of inspection results to your integrated SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA, and SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. After this function separation, choosing Complete in one of the two PODs when processing an SFC will only complete the SFC, but won't start the communication of inspection results to your integrated ERP system.

    To synchronize inspection results to your integrated SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA, and SAP S/4HANA Cloud system, you will then need to choose Submit Results.

    As of release 2411, you can continue to synchronize inspection results back to your ERP system by choosing Complete or Submit Results in the Quality Inspection Characteristic List plugin in the Work Center POD or the Operation Activity POD.

OEE MDO



OEE values are now transformed and stored in the OEE (MDO), where:
  • The MDO holds values for completed shifts
  • The MDO holds values including the individual losses per resource per shift.
  • There's no initial load available for this MDO. That means you can only access data starting with release 2411 of SAP Digital Manufacturing.
 
OEE MDO Attributes

The Manufacturing Data Object OEE has the following attributes that you can use for your analytics.

Note

Attributes that represent duration in time are measured in "seconds".

Attribute Description
AVAILABILITYThe ratio of the amount of time a resource was productive to the amount of time the resource was expected to be productive.
BREAK_DURATIONBreak time assigned for a shift.
CREATED_ATDate and time of creation of the record in OEE MDO in SAP Digital Manufacturing for execution
LAST_UPDATED_ATDate and time of last update of the record in OEE MDO in the database of SAP Digital Manufacturing for insights
LOADING_TIMEPeriod of time during which a resource is expected to be available for production minus the scheduled production interruptions, such as maintenance stops, or shift breaks.
NET_OPERATING_TIME

Amount of time required to produce the actual quantities (yield and scrap) based on the designed speed.

It is calculated using the formula: Actual Quantities Produced (yield and scrap) x Standard Rate

NET_PRODUCTION_TIMETotal Production Time minus downtimes that occurred during the Total Production Time. (Total Production Time - Downtime)
OEEOverall equipment effectiveness (= Availability x Performance x Quality)
PERFORMANCE

The ratio of the amount of time needed to produce good and bad quality items to the amount of time that would have been needed to produce these items based on the designed speed.

It is calculated using the formula: Performance = Net Operating Time / Net Production Time

PLANTPlant in which the OEE calculation is performed.
QUALITY

The ratio of time needed to produce good quality products to the total number of quantities produced (yield and scrap).

It is calculated using the formula: Value Operating Time / Net Operating Time.

QUALITY_LOSS

The number of poor quality items (scrap) multiplied by the designed speed (standard rate).

It is calculated using the formula: Quality Loss = (Actual Quantity - Quality Output) x Standard Rate

RESOURCEResource for which the OEE calculation is performed.
RUNNING_TIMELoading time minus the amount of time that was lost because of unscheduled interruptions of the production
SHIFTName or identifier of the defined shift.
SHIFT_DURATIONDuration of the shift.
SHIFT_END_DATETIMEThe date/time when the shift ends.
SHIFT_START_DATETIMEThe date/time when the shift starts.
SPEED_LOSSTime that wasn't used for production because the actual production speed was slower than planned. It is calculated using the formula (Net Production Time - Net Operating Time).
TOTAL_SCHEDULED_DOWNTotal amount of time allocated for scheduled or planned maintenance activities.
TOTAL_UNSCHEDULDED_DOWNTotal amount of time for the unscheduled or unplanned interruptions.
VALUE_OPERATING_TIME

The Value Operating Time is calculated by removing the quality loss from the Net Operating Time.

Value Operating Time = Net Operating Time - Quality Loss

WORKCENTERWork center for which the OEE calculation is performed.
 
Naming Patterns for Serialized SFCs
 
Use SerializedSfcPatternKey flag in Production Order Download (LOIPRO05 V2) workflow in the Manage Integration Workflows app to choose a custom naming pattern that is applied to SFCs when they are released to the shop floor in SAP Digital Manufacturing.
 
As a prerequisite, you must define a naming pattern and key, for example {MATERIAL_NAME}-{SERIAL_NUMBER} and 'DEFAULT', in Manage Business Settings app.
 
 
To make sure that this pattern is applied to your SFCs, you need to indicate the key in the XSLT of the Production Order Download (LOIPRO05 V2) integration workflow in the Manage Integration Workflows app as follows:
 
<SerializedSfcPatternKey>DEFAULT</SerializedSfcPatternKey>
 
Component Consumption Reconciliation
 

The Component Consumption Reconciliation app allows you to review and correct failed component consumption transactions. You can use this app to ensure accurate inventory management and to complete component consumption transactions.

When you post a component for assembly, the inventory of the component is reduced in case of successful consumption. In the event of failure for a consumption request, the inventory isn't reduced, and the failed transactions are displayed in the Component Consumption Reconciliation app.

Key Features

  • Review and correct failed transactions.

  • Display the transaction status of a component in the Inventory Status Sync column.

  • Edit the records that are in failed sync status. The other records are in display only status.

  • Edit the Inventory ID, Storage Location, Batch Number, Serial Number and Handling Unit Number, in case of failed transactions.
    Note
    You can edit only the information that's already provided in the original transaction.

  • Retry the transaction without making any change.

Define MQTT Quality of Service (QoS)

 

 The following three Quality of Service (QoS) levels enable network designers to make a choice between minimal data transmission and maximum reliability:

  • QoS 0 – This is for minimal data transmission. At this level, a message is sent just once to a subscriber, and no confirmation of receipt is sent back. Consequently, it’s impossible to tell whether subscribers have even received the message. This approach is also known as as fire and forget or at most once delivery. As this level simply assumes that a message has been delivered, messages that are to be delivered to disconnected clients that subsequently reconnect are not stored.

  • QoS 1 – The broker attempts to deliver a message to a subscriber and then waits for the subscriber to send back a confirmation that the message has been received. If the broker doesn’t receive such a confirmation within a certain time limit, it sends the message again. This method can result in the subscriber receiving the message multiple times if the broker doesn’t receive a confirmation from the subscriber in time. This is also referred to as at least once delivery.

  • QoS 2 – Both client and broker use a four-step handshake, ensuring that the message is received precisely once. This can also be described as exactly once delivery.

Line Monitor Shift Yield and Scrap POD Plugin

You can use this plugin to display an overview of yield and scrap information of a shift running multiple orders for the same material.

 

Monitor Production Processes in SAP Cloud ALM

See What´s New for more.

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.

Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Re: Transfer Project Subcontracting stock (R) to Project...

entry.title: Re: Transfer Project Subcontracting stock (R) to Project...
entry.content: hi, unfortunately the chargeback could not be carried out so easily. One possibility is to reverse the previous document. In the customer case, an EWM is connected which had carried out the outbound delivery. This meant that the documents first had to be reversed in EWM (goods issue in EWM). The material was then returned to the original storage location in the goods issue bin in EWM.

entry.title: Re: Transfer Project Subcontracting stock (R) to Project...
entry.content:
Hello,

The reversal in EWM returns all the stock posted on that delivery. That solution won't work in our scenario, we just want to recover the extra material not consumed by the subcontractor.

entry.title: S4 BAPI to Apply Vendor Block by POrg or CCode
entry.content:
In ECC, we have an auto-block vendor enhancement triggered by a BADI (in XK02, FK02, MK02) that works well. It employs a BDC to block by specific company code(s) or specific purchase org(s).

In S4, I'm programmatically applying a Central Vendor block via BTE Event 00001421 that calls BAPI_BUPA_CENTRAL_CHANGE. But I cannot find a BAPI to apply a vendor block by Purchase org or Company code. FM VENDOR_UPDATE is a direct table update and is not an option.

AI suggested this (see screen shot), but no such FM exists.

entry.title: Re: rename Odata Classes
entry.content:
This is not possible. The classes are generated by SEGW. During generation other runtime relevant artifacts like the sevice itself, service group, model etc. (IWSV, IWSG, IWOM,...) are generated as well and those classes are registered there as the service implementation. 

Renaming the classes would lead to a failing service. Additionally, every time you change something in the SEGW project, the DPC Classes will be regenerated and potential changes overwritten. Thus, never change the DPC classes and only add additional logic in the DPC_EXT classes.

entry.title: Re: SAC Overlap Line Charts?
entry.content: Hi PrathameshA, Do you know if the color of one serie of data can be change? In Vitran23 example, as some value overlap, there is the possibility to change the number color´s to match the line color? Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Business functions, capability model, Fiori apps - the commercial structure in SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: How do you like new SAP Community pages (Blogs) look & feel?
entry.content:
I don't hate it, and it's the best I can say about it.
The least thing that worries me in a properly working site is the look and feel. And this place feels like it's not properly working yet.

Give me a proper functioning "follow author" feature and I will begin to care about look and feel.

Add to it someway to find "the ABAP board" (I'm somehow a 2.0 user and need my old boards), and I'll begin to care about look and feel.

But until then, please excuse me if I give you not feedback because I'm worried SAP will work on my secondary priorities far before they do in the primary ones.

Apologies if I sound rude, it's not my intention.

Edited to make some minor grammar corrections

entry.title: Unable to retrieve the Specialists who are not on Collection worklist
entry.content:
• Inability to retrieve the details of specialists who are not on the collection worklist.
  • The SAP Standard app "Supervise Collection Worklist" discards the specialist details who are not on the collection worklist and only provides open worklist item information.
  • A custom CDS View was created from the data source I_CollectionWorklistItem to show the specialists even if they are not on the collection worklist, but the issue persists.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: S4 Upgrade: Starting the Shadow Instance after a reboot
entry.content:
Good Morning,

I am having troubles when restarting a shadow instance after a reboot.

I have sucessfully started the classical (non shadow instance) with sapcontrol.

I have started the sapstartsrv for the shadow (D02):
# su - xxxadm
% /usr/sap/XXX/SUM/abap/exe/sapstartsrv pf=/usr/sap/XXX/SUM/abap/system/XXX/SYS/profile/XXX_D02_xxxxxx -D -u xxxadm

When I start the shadown the enqueue and the message server start.
But the dispatcher does not start:
# grep -i err dev_disp
rdisp/ms_conn_error_handling: suspend/suspend timeout/resume delay = 1/300/10
*** ERROR => DpHdlDeadWp: W0 (pid 9203) died (severity=0, status=65280, action=) [dpxxwp.c 1549]
SECTION: Last Error Information
|No |Pid |Type|State |Cause|Err|Prio|Sess-Key |Sess-Type|Locked|Sem|Time |Program |Cli|User |Action |Action-Info |
*** ERROR => DpHdlDeadWp: W1 (pid 9204) died (severity=0, status=65280, action=) [dpxxwp.c 1549]
...

*** ERROR => DpHdlDeadWp: W28 (pid 9231) died (severity=0, status=65280, action=) [dpxxwp.c 1549]
*** DP_FATAL_ERROR => DpWpCheck: no more work processes
|No |Pid |Type|State |Cause|Err|Prio|Sess-Key |Sess-Type|Locked|Sem|Time |Program |Cli|User |Action |Action-Info |
|No |Pid |Type|State |Cause|Err|Prio|Sess-Key |Sess-Type|Locked|Sem|Time |Program |Cli|User |Action |Action-Info |
return errno (-17) to T0_U1
DpTmSend: errormsg without overhead: take session 0
*** ERROR => NiIWrite: SiSendV failed for hdl 33/sock 14
*** ERROR => DpTmSend: NiBufSend failed(rc=-6: NIECONN_BROKEN)->disconnect T0_U1 [dpTerminal.c 1332]
AdCvtRecToExt: opcode 60 (AD_SELFIDENT), ser 0, ex 0, errno 0
AdCvtRecToExt: opcode 40 (AD_MSBUF), ser 0, ex 0, errno 0
AdCvtRecToExt: opcode 40 (AD_MSBUF), ser 0, ex 0, errno 0

Would you know what I have missed for starting this shadow instance.

Have a nice day.

So the question is "Can we add two custom forms (which are designed in manage process form GB) on the 'Document' tab while template creation?
Looking forward the response.

#PWS 

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Update on the SAP Identity Management migration to Microsoft Entra
entry.content:
Hi @MichaelFriedrich ,

One of the crucial feature in SAP IDM is to provision identities into Active Directory. But seems Microsoft Entra ID CANNOT writeback users into on-prem AD if both are in the same DC, which will mostly be the case. Kindly check here -

https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/answers/questions/2029426/possibibility-to-sync-azure-entra-id-to-on-prem-dc

If this is true, then I doubt Entra ID is a correct alternative for SAP IDM.

entry.content: Does somebody know if the number labels color can be change in only one serie of data? SO would it be possible to change the color of the "Target Funnel" to red?

entry.title: Re: How to display staging tables created by DMC in cloud?

Hello!

You can check the uploaded instances from the staging tables in the Migrate your data fiori app under "Instances" screen, for more information you can refer the relevant SAP Help section https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/d4b13b379522491db21998994b8a8677.html?version=2408.500

Zsombor

Adding Custom Fields to Payment List Forms

Configuring the Output of Payment Proposal Lists and Payment Lists

Zsofia

entry.title: Millions of Dangerous Goods Shipments per Day Require Strict Regulation
entry.content:
Did you ever wonder why it is not allowed to include certain electronics such as power banks in checked luggage when traveling by airplane? The reason is that these include lithium-ion batteries. Airlines prohibit lithium-ion batteries in checked luggage due to safety risks outlined in international dangerous goods regulations. These batteries, while common in electronics, pose a significant fire hazard if damaged, overheated, or short-circuited. Lithium batteries are therefore classified as "Class 9 dangerous goods" due to their potential to ignite spontaneously and burn at extremely high temperatures, which is a critical concern in an aircraft's closed environment.

This classification and restriction were driven by past incidents. For example, in 2010, a UPS cargo plane carrying a large shipment of lithium batteries crashed near Dubai after an uncontrollable fire broke out in its cargo hold. Subsequent incidents on passenger flights, where smoldering batteries triggered alarms and forced emergency landings, highlighted the urgency of these restrictions. As a result, lithium batteries are only allowed in carry-on luggage, where passengers and crew can quickly respond to any malfunction. Regulations also limit the capacity of batteries that can be transported, further reducing risks associated with lithium-ion power sources during flights.

Transporting dangerous goods is not only regulated on airplanes. It is a significant and regulated part of logistics across all major modes of transport – road, rail, sea, and air. Millions of shipments of chemicals, fuels, explosives, and other hazardous materials occur daily worldwide. Road transport is the most common mode for dangerous goods, with trucks carrying approximately 60% of these materials in many countries. Rail and sea transport handle large volumes of bulk chemicals and petroleum, while air transport focuses on smaller, high-value shipments, including medical and electronic equipment.

Accidents involving dangerous goods have sometimes led to severe injuries, fatalities, and environmental damage. Dangerous goods are therefore highly regulated worldwide, with specific rules differing by region and transport mode to prioritize public safety, environmental protection, and smooth international trade. At the core of these regulations are the United Nations Model Regulations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods (often called the "UN Orange Book"), which set a global framework for classifying, packaging, labeling, and documenting hazardous materials. These model regulations help ensure consistency across international borders and are adopted and adapted by various regions for all major modes of transport. Examples of such regulations are:

  • The International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) enforces the UN’s standards for air transport, which are further refined by the International Air Transport Association (IATA) through the Dangerous Goods Regulations (DGR). IATA’s DGR is accepted globally, with requirements on packaging, labeling, and quantity limits for hazardous goods.
  • The International Maritime Organization (IMO) and its International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) Code governs hazardous materials transported by sea. The IMDG Code is followed by countries globally, including the U.S., China, and EU member states.
  • For road transport, regulations vary regionally but align with the UN framework. Europe has the Agreement concerning the International Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road (ADR), which requires specialized vehicle design, driver certification, and specific transport routes for hazardous materials. Canada and the U.S. follow the Transportation of Dangerous Goods (TDG) regulations and the Department of Transportation (DOT) Hazardous Materials Regulations, respectively, covering everything from driver training to placarding requirements for trucks.
  • Rail regulations are aligned with UN standards as well. In Europe, the Regulations concerning the International Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Rail (RID) apply, especially in countries with extensive rail networks. Japan’s Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport, and Tourism has adapted UN guidelines with additional national measures for rail transport, focusing on the densely populated areas that rail lines often pass through.

In every region, these regulations underscore the need for robust packaging, clear labeling, and specialized handling procedures, reducing the risks associated with transporting dangerous goods and supporting safer, more consistent global trade.

Non-compliance with dangerous goods regulations can lead to fines and delivery delays because police and customs often forbid non-compliant transports to continue their journeys. So how can companies ensure that their dangerous goods are packaged and transported safely and in compliance with the regulations? First, it is important to train the involved employees and to streamline the packaging and transportation processes. Second, all transports must be checked and labelled before they leave the company premises.

Software can help here, for example by automating dangerous goods processes such as the classification of dangerous goods and the creation of required documents. It can also automatically check all deliveries and transports against dangerous goods requirements. With SAP S/4HANA for product compliance, SAP offers such a software. It comes with embedded dangerous goods regulatory content for the 15 most important regulations of the world.

For more information, visit the web page and watch this video. Furthermore, SAP presents the solution at the 2025 SAP for EHS and Product Compliance Conference
